checked_queries: unsupported precompile address for Phase 2 fee calculation
negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: The audited v2.2 port lacks Video Phase 2 pricing that extracts width, height, and duration and charges executor fees by rounded-up pixel-seconds with zero validator fees.
related_but_insufficient_code: The v2.2 port registry type contains a video_fee_wei_per_pixel_second field, but no fee function uses it.
calculate_async_fees_phase2 only supports long-running HTTP, ZK, and persistent-agent Phase 2, otherwise returning unsupported precompile address.
VIDEO_CALL_PRECOMPILE is registered as a two-phase precompile, and revm has video precompile comments, but the audited port lacks builder/verifier Phase 2 video fee calculation.

notes: Create a Video Phase 2 settlement for 0x081A with encoded width, height, and duration; the current port has no calculate_video_fees_phase2 path and cannot verify the expected fee.
rationale: The fee function and verifier both route image Phase 2 to dimension extraction and `calculate_image_fees_phase2(width, height)`. Audio Phase 2 explicitly extracts `outputDurationSeconds` and uses `calculate_audio_fees_phase2(duration_seconds)`. Video Phase 2 uses `calculate_video_fees_phase2(width, height, duration_ms)` and the fee code converts duration from milliseconds to rounded-up seconds before applying the pixel-second formula. Collapsed 3 closely related historical candidate statements into the latest effective formulation.
statement: Video Phase 2 settlement pricing extracts output width, height, and duration milliseconds, rounds duration up to seconds, and charges executor fees based on pixel-seconds, with zero validator fees in Phase 2.
suggested_falsification_tests: Verify an image Phase 2 settlement whose delivery result encodes width and height; assert expected executor fee changes with `width * height` and malformed/missing dimensions reject the settlement.
Verify audio Phase 2 settlements with different encoded durations and assert executor fee scales with duration; assert a too-short delivery result is rejected.
Verify video Phase 2 settlements with fixed dimensions and varying duration, and fixed duration with varying dimensions; assert fees scale by rounded-up pixel-seconds and malformed metadata rejects.
summary: Video Phase 2 pixel-second pricing is not ported.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Verify an image Phase 2 settlement whose delivery result encodes width and height; assert expected executor fee changes with `width * height` and malformed/missing dimensions reject the settlement.
title: Video Phase 2 is per pixel-second
